content.text:
Found it: `main.js` references `state` without importing it — that would throw after login and leave the loading skeleton stuck.

[REDACTED]

input.command: cd /Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume && git diff relocation_jobs/static/js/main.js 2>&1; git log -3 --oneline -- relocation_jobs/static/js/main.js

input.description: View render.js diff for other JS bugs
Run fetch status test without xdist

content.text:
The panel stalls because `main.js` uses `state` without importing it — after login that throws and `finishLoadingProgress()` never runs. Adding the missing import.

[REDACTED]

## Problem

The panel hung on the loading skeleton because of a **JavaScript `ReferenceError`** in `main.js`.

After login, `init()` does this:

1. `showJobsLoading()` — skeleton UI
2. Loads config / countries / ATS types
3. Calls `setAdminNavVisible(Boolean(state.authState.user?.is_admin))`
4. Should load jobs and call `finishLoadingProgress()`

Step 3 used `state` but **`state` was never imported** — only `setOnUnauthorized` was imported from `state.js`. That throws after auth succeeds, so jobs never load and the skeleton never clears.

`auth.js` imports `state` correctly (login flow works); the bug only hits the **already-logged-in** path via `main.js`.

## Fix

```3:3:relocation_jobs/static/js/main.js
import { setOnUnauthorized, state } from "./state.js";
```

Hard refresh the page (Cmd+Shift+R) so the browser picks up the updated JS. No server restart needed — static assets are served with `no-store` caching.

[REDACTED]
<user_query>Briefly inform the user about the task result and perform any follow-up actions (if needed). If there's no follow-ups needed, don't explicitly say that.</user_query>

The backend smoke test finished successfully after ~38s (likely Neon waking up on first connect). All endpoints returned 200: auth, config, countries, ATS types, jobs, and fetch status.

That lines up with the real issue being the missing `state` import in `main.js` — the API was fine; the UI crashed mid-init. Hard refresh the panel and it should load normally.

[REDACTED]
